# Great Swamp Bonsai Society (GSBS) — Complete Reference
# https://www.greatswampbonsai.com

## Organization Overview

The Great Swamp Bonsai Society (GSBS) is New Jersey's premier bonsai club, founded in 1976 and named after the Great Swamp National Wildlife Refuge in Morris County, New Jersey. The club has been continuously active for 50 years and draws members from eight New Jersey counties and Eastern Pennsylvania.

GSBS holds free monthly meetings on the second Tuesday of every month from 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM at the Essex County Environmental Center in Roseland, NJ. There is no meeting in December. Meetings are open to the public — no membership, experience, or bonsai tree required to attend. Annual membership dues are $40 per year.

## Contact Information

- **Full Name:** Great Swamp Bonsai Society
- **Abbreviation:** GSBS
- **Email:** great.swampbonsai@gmail.com
- **Address:** 621-B Eagle Rock Ave, Roseland, NJ 07068
- **Venue:** Essex County Environmental Center, Main Center Classroom
- **Annual Show Venue:** Garibaldi Hall, 621-A Eagle Rock Ave (same campus)
- **Venue Phone:** (973) 228-8776
- **Facebook:** https://www.facebook.com/groups/296001945128377
- **YouTube:** https://www.youtube.com/@greatswampbonsaisociety5552
- **Website:** https://www.greatswampbonsai.com

## Officers and Key People

- **President:** Diego Garnica (also GSBS representative on NJBS Board)
- **Former President:** John Michalski (ongoing organizer, newsletter editor)
- **Treasurer:** Rick Myers
- **Artist in Residence:** Sergio Cuan — BFA from Pratt Institute, two-time Emmy Award winner, former Senior Vice President and Creative Director at Nickelodeon, received "Finest Deciduous Award" at the 4th U.S. National Bonsai Exhibition in 2014, headlined the 2024 MABS Spring Festival, illustrated "Bonsai Heresy." Leads three GSBS meetings per year.
- **Potter in Residence:** Fred Aufschlager — author of "Bonsai Containers as Ceramic Art," creates custom pots at events, hosted Saturday morning study group at his Bernardsville home
- **Webmaster:** T. Albert
- **MABS Representatives:** Leonard Schwartz, Ralf Schumann, Peter Sobol

## History

- **1976:** Founded, named after the Great Swamp National Wildlife Refuge
- **1976–2020:** Met at Haggerty Education Center, Frelinghuysen Arboretum, East Hanover Ave, Morristown, NJ
- **Meeting time evolution:** 7:00-10:00 PM -> 6:30-9:30 PM -> current 6:00-9:00 PM
- **~2005:** Mark Schmuck served as President
- **2007-2009:** John Michalski served as President (expanded guest artist program, collecting trips, MCPC partnership)
- **2009-~2019:** Diego Pablos elected President (September 2009)
- **2020-2021:** COVID-19 pandemic; Morris County Parks Commission closes Haggerty Center; outdoor meetings at members' homes; dues suspended
- **~2020-2022:** John Michalski served as President again
- **~2022:** Brief use of Morris County Library, then relocated to Essex County Environmental Center, Roseland, NJ
- **2024:** Diego Garnica elected President
- **2026:** Celebrating 50th anniversary

## Meeting Details

- **Schedule:** 2nd Tuesday of every month, 6:00–9:00 PM
- **No December meeting**
- **Informal session:** 6:00–6:30 PM (open discussion, questions, one-on-one guidance)
- **Cost to attend:** FREE
- **Open to:** Adults and children ages 12 and up
- **Guests:** Welcome to attend as many times as they like before joining
- **What to bring:** Trees, pots, tools for display, discussion, and problem-solving (nothing required)

## Directions

From I-280: Take Exit 4A, then Eisenhower Parkway South. Take the first right at the traffic light onto Eagle Rock Road. Continue half a mile — the Essex County Environmental Center is on the left. The venue is approximately 15 minutes from Morristown and accessible from Routes 280 and 80.

## 2026 Calendar

| Date | Event | Details |
|------|-------|---------|
| March 10 | Monthly Meeting | MABS tree selection, repotting, bonsai display setup |
| April 14 (afternoon) | Jonas Dupuich Workshop | ~$50, bring your own tree, register via email |
| April 14 (evening) | Monthly Meeting | Jonas Dupuich lecture and demonstration |
| April 17–19 | MABS Spring Festival | Holiday Inn Harrisburg-Hershey, Grantville PA |
| April 19 | Essex County Cherry Blossom Festival | GSBS bonsai display at Branch Brook Park |
| April 25 | Essex County Earth Day | GSBS booth 11 AM–3 PM, rain date April 26 |
| May 12 | Monthly Meeting | Air layering and propagation |
| June 6 | Annual Bonsai Show | Garibaldi Hall, 11 AM–3 PM, setup 10 AM. Free admission. |
| June 9 | Monthly Meeting | Pottery discussion with Fred Aufschlager |
| June 24–29 | Longwood Gardens Bonsai Exhibition | Kennett Square, PA — club excursion |
| July–November | Monthly Meetings | Topics TBA |
| December | No Meeting | — |

## Affiliations

- **MABS (Mid-Atlantic Bonsai Societies):** 11 member clubs across CT, DE, NY, NJ, PA, RI, and MA. Hosts annual Spring Festival. GSBS representatives: Schwartz, Schumann, Sobol.
- **NJBS (NJ Bonsai Societies):** 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Co-sponsors: Bergen Bonsai Society, Deep Cut Bonsai Society, GSBS, Pennsylvania Bonsai Society. Diego Garnica is GSBS representative on NJBS Board. Hosts annual "Art of Bonsai" exhibition.

## Guest Artists (Historical, 40+ since 2003)

GSBS has hosted 40+ internationally renowned bonsai artists including Marco Invernizzi (Italy, 2003/2006), Boon Manakitivipart (California, 2003/2004/2011, trained under Akio Kato in Japan), Horst Krekeler (Heidelberg Germany, 2003/2005, lecturer/teacher/author), Colin Lewis (Maine, 2004, published author of bonsai books), Arthur Skolnik (2004), Andy Smith (Golden Arrow Bonsai, South Dakota, 2005, Rocky Mountain Juniper specialist), Roy Nagatoshi (California, 2005, Karate Kid films), George LeBolt (New Jersey, 2005/2006, Past President MABS, 16-year Bergen Bonsai president), Min Hsuan Lo (Taiwan, 2006/2008, juniper specialist), Ted Matson (California, 2006, founded California Shohin Society), Bill Valavanis (New York, 2006, International Bonsai magazine publisher), Tony Tickle (UK, 2007, copper wire techniques), Jim Barrett (New Jersey, 2008, potter/bonsai artist), Pedro Morales (Puerto Rico, 2009/2019), Julian Adams (2009, Scots Pine specialist), Charles Ceronio (South Africa, 2010, guest from MABS), Michel Phaneuf (Canada, 2010), Michele Andolfo (Italy, 2010), Kathy Shaner (California, 2011/2022), Yasuo Mitsuya (Japan, 2011), Steve Tolley (UK, 2011), Peter Warren (UK, 2012/2013), Chase Rosade (Pennsylvania, 2012/2022, 'Mount Rushmore of American Bonsai Pioneers'), Isao Omachi (Japan, 2012), John Thompson (2012), Minoru Akiyama (Japan, 2013, youngest Prime Minister's Award winner at Sakafu), David Easterbrook (Canada, 2009/2013, Montreal Botanical Garden curator), Ron Lang (2013, bonsai potter), Suthin Sukosolvisit (Thailand/USA, 2014, Royal Bonsai Garden, bonsai since 1970), Bjorn Bjornholm (Tennessee, 2016/2022, trained 6 years under Keiichi Fujikawa at Kouka-en, certified by Japanese Bonsai Association), Mauro Stemberger (Italy, 2022, Scots Pine demo at Bonsai Shack), Marc Noelanders (Belgium, 2019, President European Bonsai Association, founded Noelanders Trophy), Will Baddeley (UK, 2022, deadwood carving specialist), Jonas Dupuich (California, 2026, Bonsai Tonight). Regular vendor: Jim Gillespie, Sho-Fu-En Bonsai, Danielsville, PA. Allshapes Bonsai Nursery, Ringoes, NJ (David & Diane Hall, Brian Zegar).

## Member Accomplishments

- Peter Michienzi won the Rosade Award for Design Excellence at MABS 2005; one of the longest-standing members; renowned yamadori collector
- Bart Thomas was selected as Curator of the Bonsai Collection at Duke Farms, Hillsborough, NJ
- Martin Schmalenberg — GSBS co-founder, Director of Asian Studies at Blairstown Academy — authored a book on North American bonsai and published a two-part article on Pinus Rigida styling in International Bonsai magazine; hosts workshops at his Stillwater Studio; organized Stone Appreciation Symposium (2012)
- Michael Markoff was First Runner-up in the Joshua Roth New Talent Competition at MABS 2010 and won the People's Choice Award at the 2011 GSBS Open House
- David Yedwab is a club specialist in tropical bonsai care and indoor bonsai techniques
- Juan Calderon — expert in design/styling and collecting; co-led Nova Scotia collecting expeditions; presented on finding design in nursery stock; vendor under "Northeast Yamadori"
- Jim Gillespie received Best Display award at the 1st US National Bonsai Show (Saratoga, 2008)

## Membership Benefits

- Expert instruction from Sergio Cuan (Artist in Residence, 3x/year)
- Guest workshops with nationally recognized bonsai artists
- Annual Bonsai Show participation
- Monthly newsletter and meeting updates
- Hands-on workshops covering repotting, wiring, pruning, carving, and styling
- Community events (Cherry Blossom Festival, Earth Day, Longwood Gardens excursion)
- A welcoming community of bonsai enthusiasts from beginner to advanced

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Do I need bonsai experience?**
No. Members range from complete beginners to advanced practitioners. Meetings include informal time for questions and one-on-one guidance.

**What age do I need to be?**
Meetings are open to adults and children ages 12 and up. Younger attendees should be accompanied by a parent or guardian.

**What if I don't have a tree?**
No tree needed to attend. Many members started by observing and learning before acquiring their first tree.

**Is there a cost to attend meetings?**
Meetings are completely free. You're welcome to attend as a guest as many times as you like before deciding to become a member.

**How much are annual dues?**
Annual dues are $40 per year. Dues support guest artist programs, show materials, and club operations.

**Can I bring my kids?**
Yes — children ages 12 and up are welcome at all meetings and events.

## Gallery

137 photos spanning 2003–2012, organized into 6 categories: Member Trees, Annual Show, Workshops, Meetings, and MABS. Includes complete step-by-step demo sequences from guest artists Andy Smith, Arthur Skolnik, Colin Lewis, Horst Krekeler, Martin Schmalenberg, and David Yedwab. Open House photos from the Frelinghuysen Arboretum era (2004-2011). Filterable by category and year.

## Resources Available on the Website

- **Monthly Bonsai Care Guide:** Month-by-month care tips for the Northeast US climate covering pruning, repotting, watering, fertilizing, pest control, and winter protection
- **Newsletter Archive:** 170+ issues (2005–2022) available for free PDF download
- **Historical Documents:** MABS festival programs, club calendars, Fred Aufschlager's "Bonsai Containers as Ceramic Art" paper, educational materials
- **Bonsai Supplies:** Links to recommended vendors
- **Learning Resources:** Curated links to bonsai education websites
- **External Links:** National organizations, regional societies, and public bonsai gardens
